shownotes: This show featured the debuts of Greenhouse Heat Death, D-Day, The Book, Polygondwanaland, and The Fourth Colour. The vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion was not performed. All Is Known was played for the first time since 2018-02-18. (69 show gap) Doom City contained a Crumbling Castle outro. Evil Death Roll was played for the first time since 2017-06-06. (50 show gap) Big Fig Wasp was played for the first time since 2016-09-22. (100 show gap)

The Coffee Cola Song (Francis Bebey) played as the band came on stage. At the start of the show, Joey says this is "the biggest show we've ever played". Before Nuclear Fusion, Joey said that he had said he wasn't going to do the vocal intro anymore, but that he'd "do it tonight". He jokingly apologized to Sam after the vocal intro. The Book was instrumental. Doom City contained a Crumbling Castle outro. Sister Ray (The Velvet Underground) was played over the PA as the setbreak music with a 15 minute countdown on the screen. Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg contained alternate lyrics and Stu on flute. Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease.

This was the band's first show in Ireland, and took place at the historic Olympia Theatre (later known as 3Olympia Theatre for sponsorship purposes). The band was supported by Mild High Club. Alex Brettin from Mild High Club sat in during King Gizzard's set for D-Day and The Book. God Is In The Rhythm was played for the first time known since 2015-06-24. (241 show gap)

This show featured the debut of Rock N’ Roll. On The Road Again (Rockets) played as the band came on stage. The vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion was not performed. Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg contained alternate lyrics and Stu on flute. Before Rock N’ Roll, Joey had to gently kick a fan off the stage. Stu later mentioned that they were recording the show that night, and that the unreleased song (Rock N’ Roll) was "kinda new". A pro-shot video of The Fourth Colour was released on the Flightless Records YouTube channel. The Fourth Colour contained a tuning session outro with a droning noise playing in the background, and eventually Cellophane teases. Robot Stop contained Master of the Universe (Hawkwind) teases and quotes, and a Hot Water tease. Malcolm Brown (formerly of Whitney) played keyboard on Beginner's Luck. Stu sat in on flute with opener Mild High Club on Sketches of Brunswick East II.

Stu sat in on flute with opener Mild High Club on Sketches of Brunswick East II.

Riff Raff (AC/DC) played as the band came on stage. Before Digital Black, Joey said "this is our biggest show in America, ever", and Stu thanked Amyl & The Sniffers (the opening act). Vomit Coffin was incomplete. Alter Me I was incomplete. Before Rattlesnake, Stu shouted out John Bonham as a "s--t bloke, good drummer", followed by a short Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) tease. The vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion was not performed. Before The Wheel Joey shouted out Sammy on the sound, Jason Galea who was working the projectors, Gaspard who was working the monitors, and "Nate Dog on the merch". Robot Stop contained Hot Water teases, and the intro contained a Trapdoor tease. Joey thanked Amyl & The Sniffers again after Gamma Knife.

This show was broadcast live by NPO 3 Extra. “Stayin' In Black” (Bee Gees + AC/DC by Wax Audio) played through the PA as the band came on stage. Following Altered Beast II, Joey joked that he preferred Lowlands over Pukkelpop (another European festival the band played the day before). Am I In Heaven? contained Alter Me, Cellophane, Rattlesnake, and Pill teases and quotes, as well as Robot Stop teases.

This show featured the debut of Year Of Our Lord. Riff Raff (AC/DC) played as the band came on stage. Cut Throat Boogie was played for the first time since 2017-12-07. (80 show gap) Let Me Mend the Past was introduced as "another old one, from the heart of [Ambrose's] 20 year old self". Eyes Like The Sky was played for the first time known since 2016-02-17. (257 show gap) The Raid was played for the first time known since 2013-11-13. (436 show gap) Only Part I of The River was performed. Wah Wah contained The River teases. Robot Stop contained a Hot Water tease. Head On/Pill contained Hot Water and Robot Stop teases as well as Alter Me, Am I In Heaven?, and Cellophane teases and quotes. After Head On/Pill, Joey thanked all the bands that played at Gizzfest 2018.
